evidence.title: README (segment 1)
evidence.excerpt: TUI Test is a framework for testing terminal applications. It provides a rich API for writing tests that interact with a terminal application across macOS, Linux, and Windows with a wide range of shells. It is built to be **fast**, **reliable**, and **easy to use**.

evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: Running tests is as simple as running TUI Test from the command line after installation:

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: **Auto-wait** TUI Test provides a rich API for interacting with the terminal. It waits for the terminal to be ready before executing commands, and it provides tooling for waiting for terminal renders before executing assertions.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: **Tracing**. Configure test retry strategy, capture stdout & stderr, and create detailed terminal snapshots to eliminate flakes.

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: **Terminal contexts**. TUI Test creates a new 'terminal context' for each test, which includes a new terminal and new underlying pty. This delivers full test isolation with zero overhead. Creating new terminal contexts only takes a handful of milliseconds.

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: **Multi-platform**. TUI Test supports testing on macOS, Linux, and Windows with a wide range of shells when installed: `cmd`, `windows powershell`, `powershell`, `bash`, `git-bash`, `fish`, `zsh`, and `xonsh`.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: **Wide-support**. TUI Test uses [xterm.js](https://xtermjs.org/) to render the terminal, which is a widely used terminal emulator in projects like [VSCode](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode) and [Hyper](https://github.com/vercel/hyper).

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: To find more TUI Test examples [check out the examples folder](./examples) or in TUI Test's [e2e tests](./test/).

## What's Changed * feat: add lifecycle hooks by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/55 * build: bump to 0.0.4 by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/56 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.3...0.0.4
## What's Changed * fix: xterm headless by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/51 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.2...0.0.3

## What's Changed * fix: ignore sourcemaps in files to transform with swc by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/48 * fix: expect timeout loading from config by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/47 * fix: only use commitlint on main by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/49 * fix: expose exit result in Terminal class by @maxpaulus43 in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/46 * build: bump to 0.0.2 by @cpendery in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/50 ## New Contributors * @maxpaulus43 made their first contribution in https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/pull/46 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test/compare/0.0.1...0.0.2
